nameMeanings.name: Saint
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Cyr, Remy.

English and French: nickname for a particularly pious individual, from Old French and Middle English saint(e), seint(e) ‘holy’ (from Latin sanctus ‘blameless, holy’). The vocabulary word was occasionally used in the Middle Ages as a personal name and this may have given rise to some instances of the surname.
English: perhaps a nickname for someone who wore, made, or sold girdles, from Middle English ceint, seint, saint ‘girdle’ (Old French ceint, Latin cinctum). Compare Center .

variants.name: Center
meanings: English and Scottish (Aberdeenshire): metonymic occupational name from Middle English, Old French ceinture, seinture ‘girdle’, denoting someone who made or sold girdles, or perhaps a nickname for someone who wore a distinctive girdle.
English: variant of   Senter   1.
Americanized form of German   Zehnder   or of some other similar (like-sounding) surname.

variants.name: Sant
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Krishan, Anagha, Anjali, Arvind, Deepak, Milind, Nilima, Ravindra, Sanjeev, Sanjiv, Vasudev.
English (Cheshire and Staffordshire): variant of   Saint  .
Italian (northeastern): variant of   Santo  .

variants.name: Sante
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Luc, Camille, Lucien.  Spanish Alejandro, Anselmo, Carlos, Emilio, Jesusa, Jorge.
French (also Santé): probably a nickname for a healthy person, from French santé ‘good health’.

Galician, Asturian-Leonese, and Spanish (southwestern): habitational name from one of several places called Sante, in Galicia, Portugal, and Asturias, from a Visigothic personal name. This surname is also found in the Philippines.

nameMeanings.name: Maur
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname for someone supposedly resembling a mouse, in appearance or timidity, from Middle High German mūs ‘mouse’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Maus ‘mouse’, one of the most common of the unflattering surnames imposed on Jews by non-Jewish government officials in 18th- and 19th-century central Europe.

variants.name: Muus
meanings: German (Hamburg): nickname for someone supposedly resembling a mouse, in appearance or timidity, from Middle High German mūs ‘mouse’. Compare Maus and Muhs .

variants.name: Muske
meanings: North German (also Müske): nickname from a diminutive of Middle Low German mūs ‘mouse’ (see Maus 1).

variants.name: Mauss
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Wilhelm.
German: variant of   Maus  .

variants.name: Meisel
meanings: German: nickname for someone supposedly resembling a mouse, in appearance or timidity, from Middle High German mūs ‘mouse’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Maus ‘mouse’, one of the most common of the unflattering surnames imposed on Jews by non-Jewish government officials in 18th- and 19th-century central Europe.

variants.name: Mousel
meanings: Americanized form of German Mäusl, from a diminutive of Maus .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Saint Maur was born about 1131, in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, John Saint Maur, was 30 and her mother, Mrs. John Saint Maur, was 25. She had at least 1 son with William Lovett.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel Saint Maur est née environ 1131, à Northamptonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Saint Maur, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Mrs. John Saint Maur, avait 25 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec William Lovett.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel Saint Maur um 1131 in Northamptonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater John Saint Maur 30 und ihre Mutter Mrs. John Saint Maur 25. Sie hatte mit William Lovett mindestens 1 Sohn.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Saint Maur è nata il circa 1131 nel Northamptonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, John Saint Maur, aveva 30 anni e sua madre, Mrs. John Saint Maur, aveva 25 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con William Lovett.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Saint Maur(이)가 대략 1131년 영국 잉글랜드 노르섬프토실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Saint Maur(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Mrs. John Saint Maur(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 William Lovett(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Saint Maur nasceu aproximadamente 1131, em Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Saint Maur, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. John Saint Maur, tinha 25 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com William Lovett.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Saint Maur родилась около 1131 г. в местности нортамптоншир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени John Saint Maur было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. John Saint Maur, 25. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от William Lovett.
